---
pagetitle: "David Maguire — Quantitative & AI Research Notebook"
page-layout: full
toc: false
---

::: {.hero}
# David Maguire

::: {.lead}
A public research notebook documenting the mathematics, models, and experiments
behind my work in quantitative finance, machine learning, and AI. The aim is
simple: to demonstrate understanding rather than claim it — a record of research
capability I am building as I develop a PhD proposal.
:::
:::

## The libraries

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4 .section-card}
#### [About](about.qmd)
Background in banking analytics, my quant-finance interests, and the AI research direction I'm heading toward.
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4 .section-card}
#### [Equation Library](equations/index.qmd)
The mathematical foundations of quant finance and machine learning — each concept taken from equation to plain English to Python.
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4 .section-card}
#### [Machine Learning Library](ml/index.qmd)
Models explained from first principles: their assumptions, how they learn, where they fail, and how they apply to markets.
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4 .section-card}
#### [Quant Research Lab](quant-lab/index.qmd)
Reproducible strategy experiments with explicit hypotheses, methods, and honestly reported results — including the ones that don't work.
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4 .section-card}
#### [AI Trading Systems](ai-systems/index.qmd)
How LLMs and AI agents could support market decision-making — and the risk controls that make such a system credible.
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4 .section-card}
#### [PhD Research](phd/index.qmd)
Developing a research proposal in the open: research questions, literature notes, methodology, and supervisor alignment.
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4 .section-card}
#### [Code Repository](code.qmd)
GitHub repositories and reproducible notebooks behind every experiment on this site.
:::

:::

## How I decide what to publish

Nothing goes on this site until I can answer six questions about it without notes:
what it means in plain English, why it matters in quant finance, what the equation
or model is actually doing, what it assumes, where it can fail, and how I would
implement it in Python. If I can't answer those, the material stays private until
I can. The goal is demonstrated understanding, not coverage.
